title: Mass. family flies home from vacation to find truck stolen from Logan Airport garage
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brendon Byrnes's truck was stolen from Logan Airport's central parking garage while his family was on vacation.
Massachusetts State Police found similarities between Byrnes's theft and another truck theft at the same garage weeks earlier.
Both incidents remain under investigation, with Byrnes suspecting his truck ended up in New Jersey based on toll records.

title: This registered nurse is running Boston for Special Olympics Massachusetts
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Gabby Peduto, a registered nurse from Wakefield, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Special Olympics Massachusetts.
Peduto is inspired by the students with complex medical needs and disabilities she works with daily.
Her participation supports the Special Olympics' mission of inclusion, empowerment, and community for individuals with disabilities.

title: North Andover police investigate ‘unauthorized’ social media comment about Kelsey Fitzsimmons case
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover Police investigate unauthorized Facebook comment regarding Kelsey Fitzsimmons's legal actions against the town.
The comment, believed to be made by an ex-employee, was removed and the department's page taken offline.
The department is auditing social media access and reviewing policies to prevent unauthorized use.

title: The Federal closes in Waltham after 7 years in business
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: The Federal, a seafood and steak restaurant in Waltham, closed after seven years due to rising costs.
Located at 74 Tower Rd., The Federal offered a menu featuring oysters, crab cakes, and shellfish towers.
The restaurant's management cited economic conditions and rising costs as reasons for the closure.

title: Your spring reading list, mapped
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston.com features a grid of 16 new spring book releases for varied reading preferences.
The curated selection ranges from fast-paced to slow-burning, accommodating different reader moods.
Readers can sign up to receive the full spring book guide and a top picks matrix via email.

title: American Express is opening a lounge with a terrace at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: American Express plans to open a 20,000 square-foot Centurion Lounge at Logan Airport's Terminal C in 2029.
The lounge will feature a terrace, premium food, local artwork, high-speed WiFi, and dedicated workstations.
Massport approved a $75 million budget for the lounge shell; American Express will invest $391 million total.

title: ‘Thank you for 8 great years!’: Fenway Johnnie’s has closed
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Fenway Johnnie's, at 96 Brookline Ave., closed after 8 years near Fenway Park.
The sports bar, known for its potato skins and burgers, announced the closure via Facebook.
Many patrons expressed surprise and nostalgia, recalling visits before Red Sox games and concerts.

title: Jayson Tatum reacts to Duke’s buzzer-beater loss to UConn: ‘I was sick’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum, Duke alumnus and chief basketball officer, felt 'sick' after Duke's loss to UConn in the Elite Eight.
Duke led by 2 points with 10 seconds left but lost after UConn's Braylon Mullins scored a buzzer-beater.
UConn advances to the Final Four and will face Illinois, following their past national titles in 2023 and 2024.

title: Boston man charged with sexually assaulting MBTA bus driver
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Michael Vandenbroeke, 41, charged with indecent assault on MBTA bus driver in the South End, pleads not guilty.
Surveillance video caught Vandenbroeke allegedly groping the driver, prompting his arrest at Nubian Square station.
Vandenbroeke has prior convictions for violent offenses; court date set for April 21 with bail set at $500.

title: The best movies on Netflix right now (April 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix's April 2026 list includes '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' starring Ralph Fiennes and Jack O’Connell.
'Atonement' (2007), featuring James McAvoy and Keira Knightley, highlights Netflix's classic film offerings this month.
Tom Cruise's original 'Mission: Impossible' from 1996 is among the classic action films available on Netflix in April.
